name: Scottish Rabbit Curry
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose a fat, fresh rabbit. Examine the kidney to test its quality.
2. Cut the rabbit into at least twelve pieces.
3. Brown these pieces in butter with onions.
4. If desired for delicate cookery, pour off the butter and add three-quarters of a pint of well-seasoned stock, one tablespoon of curry powder, and one tablespoon of flour.
5. Add six ounces of streaky bacon cut into half-inch cubes and half a dozen button onions.
6. Season with a teaspoonful of mushroom powder.
7. Simmer slowly for at least 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Add additional seasonings such as cayenne, a pinch of turmeric, or some acid to taste.
9. When the sauce is thick enough for curry dishes, pile up the rabbit pieces and pour the sauce over them.
10. Serve with plain boiled rice in a separate dish.
Fresh coco-nut can be used as an ingredient in mild curries. It should be rinsed and stewed throughout the cooking process. While green vegetables are sometimes used in curries, this recipe does not include them. Mushrooms enhance the dish, celery is beneficial, and onion is essential.
